mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real sales of new homes indicate a broad contraction in residential demand, which reduces construction sector revenues and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS coincides with positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, supporting higher corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods yield negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observations or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects strong aggregate demand and business investment, which translates into immediate positive sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods show negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases or t-stat < 1.0.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 0.5 percent annually signals supply-side stagnation and weakening manufacturing demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when INDPRO growth is below the threshold.
